bull.b: A very high recurring revenue mix provides durable revenue predictability and retention-driven growth. With subscriptions and recurring services forming the bulk of revenue, cash flows are more stable, supporting multi-quarter planning, customer expansion efforts, and steady reinvestment in product and sales capacity.

Sustained positive operating and free cash flow while still reporting GAAP losses indicates the business generates real cash from operations. This improves financial flexibility, reduces near-term financing needs, and funds product development and scaling of Decisioning Studio without relying solely on external capital

Early commercial adoption of AI products provides a durable product differentiation and new revenue streams. Measurable customer lifts demonstrate value realization, supporting upsell and price realization over time and helping Braze deepen enterprise relationships and expand wallet share.

bear.b: A marked deceleration from prior high‑growth rates weakens operating leverage and heightens execution risk. If growth remains subdued, investments in sales, product, and AI may yield lower incremental returns, making margin expansion and long‑term target achievement more challenging.

bear.t: Ongoing GAAP Losses And Negative ROE
bear.b: Persistent GAAP losses and materially negative ROE indicate the company has yet to convert revenue scale into sustainable accounting profitability. Even with improving trends, continued negative returns erode equity value and constrain options for shareholder distributions or conservative capital allocation.

bear.t: Gross Margin Pressure From Premium Messaging
bear.b: Rising premium messaging volumes and carrier fees present a structural headwind to gross margins. If higher‑cost channels or pass‑throughs grow as a share of mix, margin recovery may be limited unless Braze offsets with pricing, product mix shifts, or cost reductions—each requiring sustained execution.
